mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration and weaker corporate earnings, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production confirms real economic resilience that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize during tightening cycles when INDPRO rises, or the mechanism reverses direction in out-of-sample validation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above historical trend levels signals sustained corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 250.0, accelerating inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 250.0, or inflation thresholds lose predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth below 0.5% signals supply-side exhaustion and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize during late-cycle phases when INDPRO growth falls below 0.5%, or the signal produces false positives in early-cycle expansions.
